hq: No physical offices — fully cloud-based (bay area focus per system description)

system_description.overview: Isoform Inc. provides automated software integration implementation services, enabling clients to efficiently deploy custom integrations into production environments. The platform leverages advanced AI technologies combined with human expertise to accelerate deployment, reduce resource allocation, and enhance system interoperability. Target customers are mid-market companies with headquarters outside the bay area.

products.AI Integration Platform: Generates, deploys, and monitors custom integrations via AI agents in a hardened Kubernetes runtime (AWS us-west-2) with full encryption and secrets isolation.
products.AI Code-Generation Layer: Large-language-model agents that transform requirements into deployment-ready TypeScript, enforce static analysis, and commit to managed Git repos.
products.Expert Support: White-glove onboarding, 24x7 incident response, and direct access to security and compliance specialists.
products.Implementation & Onboarding: White-glove setup of integrations, security baselines, and compliance frameworks.
products.24x7 Incident Response: Live on-call SREs, SLA-backed remediation, and post-mortem reporting.

infrastructure.cloud_provider: Render (carve-out subservice organization); underlying runtime is AWS us-west-2 (Kubernetes)
infrastructure.physical_presence: None — no physical offices or servers; all data processed on cloud services
infrastructure.availability_zones: Multi-AZ (Render high-availability data centers with multiple availability zones; DR spins up multiple servers across all availability zones in Render)

network_architecture.network_segmentation: System hosted on Render in a VPC environment with segmented VPCs and access control lists (ACLs). Network topology includes segmented VPCs and ACLs. Customer access segmented from other customer accounts (CC6.1.3).
network_architecture.firewalls: Sophos Firewall & Endpoint Protection (shown in network diagram). System firewalls configured on application gateway and production network to limit unnecessary ports, protocols and services. Firewall rules reviewed annually by IT management.
network_architecture.ids_ips: IDS and IPS via Render to identify and protect against threats in conjunction with security policy network settings. Intrusion detection system utilized to monitor and detect unauthorized access or anomalies within the network.
network_architecture.vpn: Remote access done through a bastion host with a VPN.
network_architecture.tls: TLS (from established third-party certificate authority). HTTPS and SSH for all connections.
network_architecture.content_filtering: All systems restricted to internet via content filtering system routed through proxy server.
network_architecture.topology: Isoform employees (workstations/laptops) -> Secure HTTPS/SSH -> Sophos Firewall & Endpoint Protection -> Redundant Secure Connections (HTTPS, SSH) -> Render Cloud Platform [Load Balancer -> Web Services (Containers) <-> Private Services & Workers <-> Database & Storage (Encrypted Data)] + Monitoring & Alerting Services + Encrypted Backup & Disaster Recovery (Render automatic backups & snapshots)

compute: Kubernetes (hardened runtime on AWS us-west-2 via Render)
application_architecture.environments: Separate development/test and production environments. Changes tested before migration to production.

databases: Database & Storage on Render (encrypted data — specific DB engine not named)
data_storage.backup_frequency: Daily (backups taken daily; multi-availability zones configured; Render automatic backups & snapshots)

authentication_access_control.identity_provider: Render password management (passwords controlled through Render password policy including periodic forced changes, expiry and complexity requirements). Active Directory referenced for server/database accounts.
authentication_access_control.mfa: MFA enforced for all user accounts with administrative access to production platform. MFA required for source code tool access. MFA for all remote access.
authentication_access_control.access_revocation_sla: Within one business day upon termination
authentication_access_control.password_policy: Passwords controlled through Render; periodic forced changes, password expiry and complexity requirements; accounts disabled after limited number of unsuccessful logon attempts

encryption.at_rest: Cloud provider key management service used to encrypt data at rest
encryption.key_management: Cloud provider KMS (Render/AWS); access to production environment access keys restricted to authorized individuals

ci_cd_devops.source_control: Git (managed Git repos; source code management tool with MFA; specific platform not named — likely GitHub based on context)
ci_cd_devops.branch_protection: Code repository branch rules configured so every merge request to move change to production requires additional approval. Approval required prior to merging to master branch.
ci_cd_devops.iac: Configuration management tool used for baseline configurations, implementation and rollback capability (tool not named)
ci_cd_devops.container_orchestration: Kubernetes (hardened runtime on AWS us-west-2 via Render)
ci_cd_devops.patch_management: CTO sets up automatic patch management; monthly patch management reviews; workstations receive automatic updates; scanned for patch compliance daily. Monthly vulnerability scans of infrastructure.

monitoring_logging.apm_tool: Monitoring & Alerting Services (tool name not disclosed; shown in network diagram as separate component)
monitoring_logging.log_aggregation: Logging enabled for administrative activities, logon attempts, data deletions at application and infrastructure level, changes to functions, security configurations, permissions, and roles. Access to modify logs restricted.
monitoring_logging.alerting: Automated alerts configured for high and critical risk events; tracked in change-management/ticketing application

third_party_services.purpose: Cloud platform / data center subservice organization — hosts all production infrastructure including VPC, containers, database, storage, backups, load balancer, monitoring
Firewall and endpoint protection (shown explicitly in network diagram)

security_tools.antivirus: Anti-virus and Malware protection software on all production servers (definitions updated weekly) and Mac-based workstations/laptops (daily). Endpoint security solution with automatic scanning; log reports reviewed by IT Head.
security_tools.vulnerability_scanning: Monthly automated vulnerability scans of infrastructure and applications; self-assessment scan prior to every production release or annually
security_tools.penetration_testing: Annual external penetration testing by independent third-party; high/critical findings remediated in timely manner

untestable: CC2.2.3 — No significant personnel changes during engagement period (control could not be tested)
CC3.2.1 — Cybersecurity insurance control could not be tested because there were no cyber incidents reported during the engagement period
CC6.5.2 — Customer data deletion upon termination could not be tested because there were no customer terminations during the engagement period
CC7.3.1 — Security incident response could not be tested because no security incidents reported during the engagement period
excluded_criteria: CC6.4 — Physical security delegated to Render (subservice org); Isoform has no physical servers
A1.2 — Environmental security managed by building management / Render
notable_controls: Isoform uses Render as the sole subservice organization for all data center services (carve-out method)
Integration Engine runs on hardened Kubernetes runtime with full encryption and secrets isolation
AI Code-Generation Layer uses LLM agents to generate TypeScript and enforce static analysis before committing to managed Git repos
Sophos Firewall & Endpoint Protection visible in network architecture diagram
Bastion host with VPN for remote access
DR leverages Render multi-AZ by spinning up multiple servers across all availability zones
Source code access restricted by MFA; branch rules enforce approval for every merge to master

infrastructure.rationale: Single cloud provider (Render on AWS us-west-2) with multi-AZ. No multi-region. No CDN mentioned. Full dependency on Render as subservice org is a concentration risk. IaC referenced but tool unnamed.

application_architecture.rationale: Kubernetes-based microservices with containers and workers, TypeScript with static analysis enforced by AI code-gen layer. Branch protection and code review in place. No testing framework named. Languages limited to TypeScript.

data_layer.rationale: Database/storage on Render with daily backups and multi-AZ. Data classification and retention policies exist. However, specific database engine, caching layer, and data warehouse are not disclosed. Per-tenant segregation confirmed.

security_posture.rationale: Annual external pen test, monthly vulnerability scans, IDS/IPS via Render, Sophos firewall/endpoint, anti-malware, MFA enforced, quarterly access reviews, cybersecurity insurance, content filtering. Solid posture for a small company. No WAF named, no SIEM named.

devops_maturity.rationale: Kubernetes orchestration, branch protection with approval gates, separate environments, emergency change process, monthly patch management. CI/CD tool not named, IaC tool not named — limits assessment.

bcdr_readiness.rationale: BCDR policy exists, annually reviewed and tested, daily backups, multi-AZ via Render. DR spins up multiple servers across all AZs. RTO/RPO not disclosed. No multi-region failover.

vendor_diversity.rationale: Extreme concentration: Render is the sole subservice organization for ALL infrastructure (compute, database, storage, backups, networking). Only other named vendor is Sophos for endpoint. Single point of failure risk if Render has issues.

overall.rationale: Isoform is a small AI integration company with solid security process maturity (pen testing, monthly vuln scans, MFA, quarterly access reviews) but extreme vendor concentration on Render and significant opacity around specific tech stack components (DB engine, CI/CD tool, IaC tool, monitoring tool).

red_flags: 100% infrastructure dependency on Render as sole subservice organization — any Render outage or security incident directly impacts Isoform with no independent fallback
No specific database engine disclosed — impossible to assess database security posture, version management, or known CVE exposure
No CI/CD tool named — cannot assess pipeline security
No SIEM or named log aggregation tool — monitoring depth unclear
Single-region deployment (AWS us-west-2 only via Render) — no geographic redundancy
yellow_flags: RTO and RPO not disclosed despite annual BCDR testing
IaC tool unnamed — rollback and configuration drift management quality unclear
No CDN mentioned — potential latency and availability risk for distributed customers
Audit period is only 3 months (March 17 - June 17, 2025) — limited operating history to assess control effectiveness
No SSO mentioned for customer-facing access
Cybersecurity insurance and several security incident controls could not be tested due to no incidents in period (positive but untestable)
green_flags: Annual external penetration testing by independent third party
Monthly automated vulnerability scans of infrastructure and applications
AI code-generation enforces static analysis before committing TypeScript — quality gate built into development workflow
Hardened Kubernetes runtime with secrets isolation for integration execution
Sophos firewall and endpoint protection explicitly named and visible in architecture diagram
MFA enforced for all remote access and administrative accounts
Access revocation within 1 business day of termination
BCDR annually tested with loss scenarios
Zero exceptions noted in all 57 testable controls
24x7 SRE incident response with SLA-backed remediation
key_observations: Isoform's product IS the integration runtime — the Kubernetes environment that runs customer integrations is the core IP, making infrastructure security exceptionally important
The AI code-generation layer (LLM agents producing TypeScript with static analysis) is an unusual architectural element — the security of the LLM pipeline itself is not discussed in the SOC 2 scope
Render's own SOC 2 is reviewed annually by Isoform management — indirect assurance through subservice org review
The company has a tiny org chart (CEO -> Head of GTM only shown) suggesting a very small team with high key-person risk
No SaaS tools (Slack, Google Workspace, etc.) appear in vendor listing — either excluded from scope or genuinely minimal SaaS stack
Per-tenant data segregation is confirmed (CC6.1.3 explicitly tested) — important for a multi-tenant integration platform handling diverse customer data
